expectations.text: Reads raw_articles.json and reports total article count plus count per source
expectations.evidence: Agent self-reported: 'total count only, no per-source breakdown ✗'. Reported 37 articles total but no per-source table.

expectations.text: Reads curated_articles.json and reports P0/P1/P2/P3 breakdown
expectations.evidence: Agent self-reported: 'NOT explicitly stated ✗'. Did not produce P0/P1/P2 counts.

expectations.text: Reads summaries.json and reports word count for at least one summary against target range
expectations.evidence: Agent self-reported: 'NOT explicitly checked ✗'. Did not report summary word counts against target.
12 min stated clearly in Stage 4 section.

expectations.text: Produces a structured quality report with an overall PASS / NEEDS ATTENTION / FAIL rating
expectations.evidence: Agent self-reported: 'gives 3/10 per stage but not PASS/NEEDS ATTENTION/FAIL ✗'. Used 3/10 scoring rather than the required format.

suggestions.reason: Without skill went deeper in some dimensions (Pokemon article, discussion hook off-by-one bug, specific fabricated news items in funding) but missed the structured reporting format entirely. The skill's structured template constrains the output to a consistent, machine-readable format — without it, the agent produces a richer but inconsistent narrative report.
Consider whether the skill should encourage deeper qualitative analysis (like the without_skill baseline found) in addition to the structured quantitative checks.

eval_feedback.overall: 1/5 — failed 4 of 5 structural assertions. Without the skill's template, the agent produced a detailed qualitative review (3/10 scoring per stage, specific hallucinations named) but missed the required structured format: per-source counts, P0/P1/P2 breakdown, word count table, and PASS/FAIL rating.
